rationale: Re-verified fix commit 81784f56 against round-1 1ad0d796, pinned reads to 81784f56, all scratch work in throwaway git-worktrees under /tmp removed after use, this worktree's git status stayed clean throughout. Item 1 (shell injection via slug/spec): re-extracted the actual validateArgs/validatePathElement/validateShellSafe from the post-fix file and drove it with the identical payload plus four isolated pure-shell-metachar payloads (semicolon/backtick/pipe/ampersand) containing NO whitespace and NO path separator -- each throws 'contains a shell metacharacter', proving validateShellSafe itself is the guard, not an incidental whitespace/path-sep catch. ADDRESSED, robustly. Item 2 (hollow positive-enumeration): re-ran my EXACT original reproduction (injecting a real, non-comment 'mindspec panel create' literal into ms-panel.js:282's prompt string) against the TRUE pre-fix commit 1ad0d796 in a clean scratch worktree -- confirmed it silently PASSES (does not reproduce the fix author's claim; the injected text falls into a scanner gap between two literals rather than into an over-broad Contains-matching blob, so 'mindspec' never appears in any scanned lit.value at all -- a genuine miss, not a coincidental save). The same injected literal against post-fix 81784f56 correctly FAILS the test, and the reported literal value is the exact clean expected string (byte-for-byte, no garbling), proving the catch comes from restored, correct string-boundary tracking rather than a lucky substring match on a corrupted span. Full test suite + build green on post-fix. ADDRESSED, and now non-coincidental.
findings: Item 1 ADDRESSED: validateArgs (post-fix ms-panel.js) now calls validateShellSafe on both slug and spec in addition to validatePathElement; validatePathElement itself also gained a whitespace guard. Verified with the original combined payload (both slug and spec now throw) AND with four isolated pure-metacharacter payloads (no whitespace/path-sep) that only validateShellSafe could catch -- all four throw 'contains a shell metacharacter'.

Item 2 ADDRESSED and confirmed non-coincidental: re-ran my exact round-1 reproduction against the TRUE pre-fix commit 1ad0d796 (not a re-derivation) -- confirmed the injected literal at ms-panel.js:282 silently passes TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet there, matching my original round-1 finding exactly (my original claim reproduces; I do not concede the fix author's alternate reproduction, which does not match what I observe). Root cause on inspection: the desync doesn't merge everything into one Contains-matching blob -- the injected substring falls into a scanner gap (untracked as any literal at all) between offsets ~11558 and ~11629, so 'mindspec' never appears in any lit.value the test inspects. Against post-fix 81784f56, the same injection now fails the test with a byte-exact, ungarbled literal value reported -- the \x60 escape genuinely restores correct quote-boundary tracking rather than papering over the gap.

Bonus hardening also landed in the delta (not required by my round-1 items, noted for completeness): validateMix now enum-checks mix[].family against {"claude","codex"} at validateArgs time, failing closed before Step 2 rather than only inside runSlot's later throw.

go build ./... and go test -count=1 ./plugins/... ./internal/setup/... green on the post-fix commit with the file restored to its clean (non-injected) state; only the two ms-panel.js copies changed in the diff, both identically.
